title: The Myth of the Post-Documentation Era
content: There is a growing sentiment in engineering circles right now that documentation is a relic of the... Navigation menu Search Powered by Algolia Search Add reaction Like Unicorn Exploding Head Raised Hands Fire Jump to Comments Save Boost More... Copy link Copy link Copied to Clipboard Share to X Share to LinkedIn Share to Facebook Share to Mastodon Share Post via... Report Abuse Ben Halpern Posted on Jul 13 # ai # documentation # opensource # codequality The gap between code logic and human intent There is a growing sentiment in engineering circles right now that documentation is a relic of the past. The argument usually goes something like this: We’re living in the era of agent-driven development. If an AI agent can read the raw source code or parse an OpenAPI specification instantly, why waste human engineering hours writing prose? Code churns too fast anyway, and human-written docs are outdated the second they’re committed. It’s an attractive, black-and-white view of the world. It’s also completely wrong. Chasing strict determinism in your source of truth is a pipe dream. Code and specs tell a system how something works, but they are fundamentally incapable of explaining why it was built that way in the first place. The Intent Gap: Why Code Isn't Enough Even if you’re building entirely for a downstream consumer of AI agents, there is a massive, structural gap between a raw API specification and an operational reality. Agents are phenomenal at pattern matching and syntax execution, but they struggle with architectural philosophy and human intent. We still need words to co
eventKey: myth-post-documentation-era
normalizedTitle: the myth of the post-documentation era

title: DMS 1.5 "The Wolverine" Released
content: The fourth major release of DMS - Frame Mode, DankCalendar integration, Hyprland Lua config, enhanced MangoWM support, and a lot more. Skip to main content DMS 1.5 "The Wolverine" is here — Frame Mode, DankCalendar integration, Hyprland Lua, and a lot more. Read the announcement DANK LINUX Install Docs Plugins & Themes Blog 1.5 1.6 1.5 1.4 1.2 Recent Posts 2026 DMS 1.5 "The Wolverine" Released Introducing DankCalendar DMS 1.4 "Saffron Bloom" Released DMS 1.2 "Spicy Miso" Released 2025 New Desktop Widgets & Plugins Coming in DMS 1.2 DMS 1.5 "The Wolverine" Released June 23, 2026 · 56 min read Dank Linux Team DMS 1.5 DankMaterialShell (DMS) 1.5 "The Wolverine" is here! This release introduces Frame Mode, a new connected surface that ties the whole shell together, native DankCalendar integration, Hyprland Lua config and enhanced MangoWM support, a reworked Material 3-like shadow system, and hundreds of fixes and improvements across DMS. DMS 1.5 adds the option to draw the shell surfaces as one connected surface, tying modals (such as the launcher), popouts, and notifications into the bar and frame. DMS 1.5 brings native support for DankCalendar ( docs ) as a calendar backend over IPC. This is a major new application addition that unifies Local, Google, Microsoft, CalDAV, and iCloud accounts into a single agenda. When the dcal daemon is active, DMS detects it and establishes a bidirectional IPC connection. That connection enables several shell integrations: The DMS calendar provider is configurable for backwards compatibility with Khal , so users can continue to use the khal ba

title: 用 codex 半个月， 轻松实现一个跨平台的 PDF 软件
content: OpenAI - @wuruxu - 用 chromium 150 版本的 UI views 作为跨平台的 UI 框架，pdfium 为 pdf 渲染核心 现在实现跨平台，不需要收费的 QT ，也可以很稳的实现跨平台软件 用 chromium 150 版本的 UI views 作为跨平台的 UI 框架，pdfium 为 pdf 渲染核心 现在实现跨平台，不需要收费的 QT ，也可以很稳的实现跨平台软件 codex 足够强大，每次改动 几乎很少出错 欢迎试用 https://github.com/libcr/crpdf/releases
eventKey: codex-半个月-轻松实现一个跨平台的-pdf
normalizedTitle: 用 codex 半个月 轻松实现一个跨平台的 pdf 软件
summary:
用 chromium 150 版本的 UI views 作为跨平台的 UI 框架，pdfium 为 pdf 渲染核心 现在实现跨平台，不需要收费的 QT ，也可以很稳的实现跨平台软件
codex 足够强大，每次改动 几乎很少出错

欢迎试用 https:…
